reforms.description: The Sarbanes-Oxley Act was enacted to enhance corporate governance and accountability in response to financial scandals, increasing penalties for fraud and improving disclosure requirements for public companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing corporate governance can lead to increased investor confidence and potentially higher levels of investment in innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The act has been in force since its enactment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in corporate governance.
reforms.source_quote: The 2002 Sarbanes-Oxley Act stiffened penalties for fraud, enhanced both auditor independence and regulation of the accounting profession and increased both disclosure requirements for public companies.

reforms.description: Proposals have been made to tighten the regulation and supervision of government-sponsored housing finance companies, Freddie Mac and Fannie Mae, to eliminate their special status and implicit government guarantees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While tightening regulation may reduce risks, it could also limit the availability of financing for innovation in housing.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposals have been made but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposals represent a significant change in the regulatory framework for housing finance.
reforms.source_quote: The Administration has made proposals to improve regulation and supervision of government-sponsored housing finance companies.

reforms.description: The Research and Experimentation Tax Credit, originally enacted in 1981, has been extended multiple times, with the most recent extension scheduled to expire in June 2004. This tax credit incentivizes firms to invest in R&D activities by providing a tax reduction based on their research expenditures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit encourages firms to increase their R&D spending, which can lead to greater innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit has been extended and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax credit is a significant policy tool for promoting R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: The Research and Experimentation Tax Credit, usually referred to as the R&D tax credit, has since been extended 10 times, most recently in November 1999, and is currently scheduled to expire in June 2004.

reforms.description: The Telecommunications Act of 1996 aimed to spur local service competition by allowing Bell operating companies to enter long-distance services if local service was open to competition, thereby linking local and long-distance market dynamics to foster innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering competition, the act encourages innovation in telecommunications services and infrastructure.
reforms.status_evidence: The act has been in force since its enactment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The act represents a major overhaul of telecommunications regulation.
reforms.source_quote: The Telecommunications Act of 1996 began the national effort to spur local service competition.
